---
title: "Projects"
css: styles/projects.css
---

::: project-card
::: project-header
::: project-title-block
### Predicting Flight Delay Severity at ORD, JFK & ATL
[Distributed Computing for Big Data · 2026]{.project-meta}
:::
::: project-tags
[Python]{.tag} [Apache Spark]{.tag} [MLlib]{.tag} [Ensemble Learning]{.tag} [GCP]{.tag}
:::
:::

Framed flight delay prediction as a four-class severity classification problem (no delay, minor, major, severe/cancellation) using five years of US Bureau of Transportation Statistics data covering ~3.5M flights across three major hub airports. Built and evaluated six model configurations in Apache Spark MLlib — logistic regression, decision tree, and KNN baselines alongside bagging, boosting, and a stacking ensemble — comparing both predictive performance and distributed scalability metrics such as training time, speedup ratio, and memory usage.
:::

::: project-card
::: project-header
::: project-title-block
### Predictive Forecasting of Citi Bike Station Demand in NYC
[Deep Learning · 2026]{.project-meta}
:::
::: project-tags
[Python]{.tag} [Graph Neural Networks]{.tag} [PyTorch]{.tag} [Spatio-temporal]{.tag}
:::
:::

Developed deep learning models to forecast hourly bike inflow and outflow at 700+ NYC Citi Bike stations, supporting smarter rebalancing logistics and reducing empty or full-station scenarios. Constructed a spatial graph from station coordinates and flow patterns, then modeled temporal dependencies using GCN, GAT, STGCN, and graph transformer architectures. Incorporated NYC hourly weather data as auxiliary features and benchmarked against LSTM, GRU, and ARIMA baselines across ~30M trip records spanning 2013–2023.
:::

::: project-card
::: project-header
::: project-title-block
### F1 Popularity Growth & Sustainability Analysis
[Managining and Visualising Data · 2025/2026]{.project-meta}
:::
::: project-tags
[Python]{.tag} [Pandas]{.tag} [Plotly]{.tag} [Seaborn]{.tag} [REST APIs]{.tag}
:::
:::
Investigated the relationship between F1's global popularity growth and its sustainability challenges across multiple decades of race calendar data. Integrated circuit location, attendance, and YouTube engagement data from the official F1 channel to explore how calendar expansion, track tenure, and travel intensity have evolved alongside fan demand. Analysed the impact of Drive to Survive on digital engagement trends and examined whether calendar geography has been optimised in line with F1's net-zero-by-2030 commitment.
:::


::: project-card
::: project-header
::: project-title-block
### Interactive F1 Qualifying Analysis
[Statistics SYE (Honors) - St. Lawrence University · 2024–2025]{.project-meta}
:::
::: project-tags
[R]{.tag} [Shiny]{.tag} [Data Viz]{.tag}
:::
:::

Built an interactive Shiny application for exploring F1 telemetry and qualifying data across the 2018–2024 seasons. Users can compare two drivers head-to-head within a race or track a single driver's performance over time through customizable, publication-quality visualizations.

[{{< fa brands github >}} f1-animate](https://github.com/norah-kuduk/f1-animate){.project-link}
:::

::: project-card
::: project-header
::: project-title-block
### AI-Augmented Physical Therapy App
[Computer Science SYE (Honors) - St. Lawrence University · 2024–2025]{.project-meta}
:::
::: project-tags
[Flutter]{.tag} [PostgreSQL]{.tag} [Docker]{.tag}
:::
:::

Developed a cross-platform app for athletes and individuals managing chronic injuries, featuring scheduling, calendar views, and a customizable exercise library. Designed a relational data model for exercises, routines, and user progress, backed by a PostgreSQL database in Docker. Leveraged AI-assisted development workflows (ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot) throughout the build.
:::
